"""Tests for src/listener.py — parsing and dispatch logic."""

from __future__ import annotations

import email
import email.policy
import textwrap
from email.message import EmailMessage
from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from listener import (
    Config,
    dispatch_task,
    extract_spec,
    get_status,
    parse_subject,
    process_message,
    write_stop_sentinel,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_config(tmp_path: Path) -> Config:
    ralph_sh = tmp_path / "ralph.sh"
    ralph_sh.write_text("#!/bin/bash\necho done\n")
    ralph_sh.chmod(0o755)

    return Config(
        imap_host="imap.example.com",
        imap_port=993,
        imap_user="agent@example.com",
        imap_pass="secret",
        smtp_host="smtp.example.com",
        smtp_port=465,
        smtp_user="agent@example.com",
        smtp_pass="secret",
        workspace=tmp_path,
        ralph_sh=ralph_sh,
        poll_interval=5,
    )


def make_email(
    subject: str,
    body: str,
    from_addr: str = "user@example.com",
    md_attachment: str | None = None,
) -> bytes:
    msg = EmailMessage()
    msg["From"] = from_addr
    msg["To"] = "agent@example.com"
    msg["Subject"] = subject
    msg.set_content(body)
    if md_attachment is not None:
        msg.add_attachment(
            md_attachment.encode(),
            maintype="text",
            subtype="markdown",
            filename="spec.md",
        )
    return msg.as_bytes()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# parse_subject
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestParseSubject:
    def test_task_with_description(self) -> None:
        prefix, desc = parse_subject("[task] deploy the harness")
        assert prefix == "task"
        assert desc == "deploy the harness"

    def test_stop(self) -> None:
        prefix, desc = parse_subject("[stop]")
        assert prefix == "stop"
        assert desc == ""

    def test_status(self) -> None:
        prefix, desc = parse_subject("[status]")
        assert prefix == "status"
        assert desc == ""

    def test_case_insensitive(self) -> None:
        prefix, _ = parse_subject("[TASK] something")
        assert prefix == "task"

    def test_unrecognized(self) -> None:
        prefix, raw = parse_subject("Hello world")
        assert prefix == ""
        assert raw == "Hello world"

    def test_task_no_description(self) -> None:
        prefix, desc = parse_subject("[task]")
        assert prefix == "task"
        assert desc == ""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# extract_spec
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestExtractSpec:
    def test_plain_body(self) -> None:
        raw = make_email("[task] test", "# My Spec\n\nDo the thing.")
        msg = email.message_from_bytes(raw, policy=email.policy.default)
        spec = extract_spec(msg)
        assert "My Spec" in spec
        assert "Do the thing" in spec

    def test_md_attachment_preferred_over_body(self) -> None:
        raw = make_email(
            "[task] test",
            "Ignore this body.",
            md_attachment="# Attachment Spec\n\nUse this.",
        )
        msg = email.message_from_bytes(raw, policy=email.policy.default)
        spec = extract_spec(msg)
        assert "Attachment Spec" in spec
        assert "Ignore this body" not in spec

    def test_empty_email(self) -> None:
        msg = EmailMessage()
        msg["From"] = "x@example.com"
        msg["Subject"] = "[task] empty"
        spec = extract_spec(msg)
        assert spec == ""

    def test_multipart_extracts_plain(self) -> None:
        raw = make_email("[task] multi", "Plain text spec here.")
        msg = email.message_from_bytes(raw, policy=email.policy.default)
        spec = extract_spec(msg)
        assert "Plain text spec here" in spec


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# write_stop_sentinel
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWriteStopSentinel:
    def test_creates_file(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        write_stop_sentinel(cfg)
        stop_file = tmp_path / ".stop"
        assert stop_file.exists()
        assert "stop" in stop_file.read_text()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# get_status
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestGetStatus:
    def test_no_stop_sentinel(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        status = get_status(cfg)
        assert "No stop sentinel" in status

    def test_with_stop_sentinel(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        (tmp_path / ".stop").write_text("stop\n")
        status = get_status(cfg)
        assert "Stop sentinel is present" in status

    def test_includes_git_section(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        status = get_status(cfg)
        assert "Recent commits" in status or "Could not read git log" in status


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# dispatch_task
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestDispatchTask:
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_writes_task_file_and_runs_ralph(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        spec = "# Deploy\n\nRun the thing."
        summary = await dispatch_task(cfg, spec, "deploy the thing")

        task_file = tmp_path / "TASK.md"
        assert task_file.exists()
        assert "Deploy" in task_file.read_text()
        assert "deploy the thing" in summary
        assert "Ralph loop" in summary

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_non_zero_exit_noted_in_summary(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        ralph_sh = tmp_path / "ralph.sh"
        ralph_sh.write_text("#!/bin/bash\nexit 1\n")
        ralph_sh.chmod(0o755)
        cfg = Config(
            imap_host="x",
            imap_port=993,
            imap_user="u",
            imap_pass="p",
            smtp_host="x",
            smtp_port=465,
            smtp_user="u",
            smtp_pass="p",
            workspace=tmp_path,
            ralph_sh=ralph_sh,
        )
        summary = await dispatch_task(cfg, "spec", "desc")
        assert "exited with code 1" in summary

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_writes_to_implementation_plan(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        plan = tmp_path / "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
        plan.write_text("# Implementation Plan\n\n## Current Focus\n\nexisting task\n")
        await dispatch_task(cfg, "# Spec\n\nDo it.", "email task desc")
        content = plan.read_text()
        assert "email task desc" in content
        assert "existing task" in content

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_timeout_kills_process_and_returns_message(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        ralph_sh = tmp_path / "ralph.sh"
        ralph_sh.write_text("#!/bin/bash\nsleep 999\n")
        ralph_sh.chmod(0o755)
        cfg = Config(
            imap_host="x", imap_port=993, imap_user="u", imap_pass="p",
            smtp_host="x", smtp_port=465, smtp_user="u", smtp_pass="p",
            workspace=tmp_path, ralph_sh=ralph_sh, ralph_timeout=1,
        )
        summary = await dispatch_task(cfg, "spec", "slow task")
        assert "timed out" in summary


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# process_message (integration-style with mocks)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestProcessMessage:
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_task_prefix_triggers_dispatch_and_reply(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        raw = make_email("[task] build listener", "# Spec\n\nDo it.", "user@example.com")

        with (
            patch("listener.dispatch_task", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value="Work done.") as mock_dispatch,
            patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply,
        ):
            await process_message(cfg, raw)

        mock_dispatch.assert_awaited_once()
        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()
        _, reply_to, _, body = mock_reply.call_args[0]
        assert "Work done" in body

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_stop_prefix_writes_sentinel_and_replies(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        raw = make_email("[stop]", "halt please", "user@example.com")

        with patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)

        assert (tmp_path / ".stop").exists()
        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_status_prefix_sends_status_reply(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        raw = make_email("[status]", "", "user@example.com")

        with patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)

        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()
        _, _, _, body = mock_reply.call_args[0]
        assert "agentx status" in body

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_unrecognized_prefix_ignored(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        raw = make_email("Hello there", "random email", "user@example.com")

        with patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)

        mock_reply.assert_not_awaited()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_allowed_sender_passes(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = Config(
            **{**make_config(tmp_path).__dict__,
               "allowed_senders": frozenset(["user@example.com"])},
        )
        raw = make_email("[status]", "", "user@example.com")
        with patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)
        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_unauthorised_sender_rejected(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = Config(
            **{**make_config(tmp_path).__dict__,
               "allowed_senders": frozenset(["allowed@example.com"])},
        )
        raw = make_email("[task] do something", "spec", "evil@example.com")
        with patch("listener.dispatch_task", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_dispatch:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)
        mock_dispatch.assert_not_awaited()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_name_bracket_addr_format_parsed(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = Config(
            **{**make_config(tmp_path).__dict__,
               "allowed_senders": frozenset(["user@example.com"])},
        )
        raw = make_email("[status]", "", "User Name <user@example.com>")
        with patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply:
            await process_message(cfg, raw)
        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_task_with_empty_spec_sends_error_reply(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
        msg = EmailMessage()
        msg["From"] = "user@example.com"
        msg["Subject"] = "[task] oops"
        raw = msg.as_bytes()

        with (
            patch("listener.dispatch_task", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_dispatch,
            patch("listener.send_reply", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_reply,
        ):
            await process_message(cfg, raw)

        mock_dispatch.assert_not_awaited()
        mock_reply.assert_awaited_once()
        _, _, _, body = mock_reply.call_args[0]
        assert "Could not extract" in body


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config.from_env
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestConfigFromEnv:
    def test_requires_imap_user(self, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
        monkeypatch.delenv("IMAP_USER", raising=False)
        monkeypatch.delenv("IMAP_PASS", raising=False)
        monkeypatch.delenv("SMTP_USER", raising=False)
        monkeypatch.delenv("SMTP_PASS", raising=False)
        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="IMAP_USER"):
            Config.from_env()

    def test_loads_from_env(self, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        monkeypatch.setenv("IMAP_USER", "u@example.com")
        monkeypatch.setenv("IMAP_PASS", "pass1")
        monkeypatch.setenv("SMTP_USER", "u@example.com")
        monkeypatch.setenv("SMTP_PASS", "pass2")
        monkeypatch.setenv("WORKSPACE_PATH", str(tmp_path))
        cfg = Config.from_env()
        assert cfg.imap_user == "u@example.com"
        assert cfg.workspace == tmp_path

    def test_allowed_senders_parsed_from_env(self, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        monkeypatch.setenv("IMAP_USER", "u@example.com")
        monkeypatch.setenv("IMAP_PASS", "pass1")
        monkeypatch.setenv("SMTP_USER", "u@example.com")
        monkeypatch.setenv("SMTP_PASS", "pass2")
        monkeypatch.setenv("WORKSPACE_PATH", str(tmp_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("AGENTX_ALLOWED_SENDERS", "a@example.com, B@EXAMPLE.COM")
        cfg = Config.from_env()
        assert cfg.allowed_senders == frozenset(["a@example.com", "b@example.com"])

"""Tests for src/send_task.py — task email dispatch."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

import pytest

from listener import Config
from send_task import send_task


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_config(tmp_path: Path) -> Config:
    ralph_sh = tmp_path / "ralph.sh"
    ralph_sh.write_text("#!/bin/bash\necho done\n")
    ralph_sh.chmod(0o755)
    return Config(
        imap_host="imap.example.com",
        imap_port=993,
        imap_user="agent@example.com",
        imap_pass="secret",
        smtp_host="smtp.example.com",
        smtp_port=465,
        smtp_user="sender@example.com",
        smtp_pass="smtpsecret",
        workspace=tmp_path,
        ralph_sh=ralph_sh,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_subject_has_task_prefix(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "deploy harness", "# Deploy\nRun docker compose up")
        msg = mock_send.call_args.args[0]
        assert msg["Subject"] == "[task] deploy harness"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_to_address_is_imap_user(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "self task", "spec body")
        msg = mock_send.call_args.args[0]
        assert msg["To"] == cfg.imap_user


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_from_address_is_smtp_user(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "task", "spec")
        msg = mock_send.call_args.args[0]
        assert msg["From"] == cfg.smtp_user


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_uses_smtp_credentials(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "task", "spec")
        kw = mock_send.call_args.kwargs
        assert kw["hostname"] == cfg.smtp_host
        assert kw["port"] == cfg.smtp_port
        assert kw["username"] == cfg.smtp_user
        assert kw["password"] == cfg.smtp_pass
        assert kw["use_tls"] is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_description_preserved(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "install ollama on VPS", "spec content")
        msg = mock_send.call_args.args[0]
        assert msg["Subject"] == "[task] install ollama on VPS"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_task_calls_aiosmtplib_once(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    cfg = make_config(tmp_path)
    with patch("send_task.aiosmtplib.send", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_send:
        await send_task(cfg, "task", "spec")
        assert mock_send.call_count == 1

## Summary

I've read both test files. Here's the full content and analysis of what is tested and how mocking is used:

## /home/ralph/workspace/src/tests/test_listener.py

This comprehensive test file contains 377 lines testing the email listener functionality. It tests the following components:

### **Functions Tested:**
1. **parse_subject()** - Parses email subjects for command prefixes like `[task]`, `[stop]`, `[status]`
2. **extract_spec()** - Extracts task specifications from email bodies or markdown attachments
3. **write_stop_sentinel()** - Creates a `.stop` file to halt the agent
4. **get_status()** - Returns agent status including git information
5. **dispatch_task()** - Writes task files and executes the Ralph agent
6. **process_message()** - Main message processing logic with sender validation
7. **Config.from_env()** - Configuration loading from environment variables

### **Mocking Usage:**
- **AsyncMock** for async functions like `dispatch_task` and `send_reply`
- **MagicMock** for general mocking needs
- **patch()** decorator to mock external dependencies
- Mocks the `send_reply` function to test email responses without actual SMTP calls
- Mocks `dispatch_task` to test message processing without executing actual tasks

### **Key Test Scenarios:**
- Subject parsing with various prefixes (task, stop, status)
- Email content extraction (body vs markdown attachment preference)
- File system operations (creating stop sentinels, task files)
- Process execution with timeouts and error handling
- Sender authentication and authorization
- Integration testing of the full message processing pipeline

## /home/ralph/workspace/src/tests/test_send_task.py

This focused test file contains 95 lines testing the task email sending functionality.

### **Function Tested:**
- **send_task()** - Sends self-tasking emails to the agent's own inbox

### **Mocking Usage:**
- **AsyncMock** for the `aiosmtplib.send` function
- **patch()** to mock SMTP operations without actually sending emails
- Captures and validates the email message structure and SMTP parameters

### **Key Test Scenarios:**

1→"""
     2→agentx email listener
     3→
     4→Polls agentx@runggp.com IMAP inbox for task emails, dispatches ralph.sh,
     5→and replies with a work summary.
     6→
     7→Usage:
     8→    uv run --env-file /opt/agentx/secrets.env src/listener.py
     9→
    10→Subject prefixes:
    11→    [task] <description>   — extract spec from body/.md attachment, run ralph
    12→    [stop]                 — write .stop sentinel to workspace; loop exits cleanly
    13→    [status]               — reply with current loop state and recent git commits
    14→"""
    15→
    16→from __future__ import annotations
    17→
    18→import asyncio
    19→import email
    20→import email.policy
    21→import logging
    22→import os
    23→import subprocess
    24→import sys
    25→from dataclasses import dataclass
    26→from email.message import EmailMessage
    27→from pathlib import Path
    28→
    29→import aioimaplib
    30→import aiosmtplib
    31→
    32→log = logging.getLogger("agentx.listener")
    33→
    34→
    35→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→# Configuration
    37→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    40→class Config:
    41→    imap_host: str
    42→    imap_port: int
    43→    imap_user: str
    44→    imap_pass: str
    45→    smtp_host: str
    46→    smtp_port: int
    47→    smtp_user: str
    48→    smtp_pass: str
    49→    workspace: Path
    50→    ralph_sh: Path
    51→    poll_interval: int = 30
    52→    allowed_senders: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
    53→    ralph_timeout: int = 1800
    54→
    55→    @classmethod
    56→    def from_env(cls) -> "Config":
    57→        def require(key: str) -> str:
    58→            val = os.environ.get(key, "")
    59→            if not val:
    60→                raise RuntimeError(f"Required env var {key!r} is not set")
    61→            return val
    62→
    63→        workspace = Path(os.environ.get("WORKSPACE_PATH", "/opt/agentx"))
    64→        ralph_sh = Path(os.environ.get("RALPH_SH", str(workspace / "ralph.sh")))
    65→
    66→        raw_senders = os.environ.get("AGENTX_ALLOWED_SENDERS", "")
    67→        allowed_senders: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    68→            s.strip().lower() for s in raw_senders.split(",") if s.strip()
    69→        )
    70→
    71→        return cls(
    72→            imap_host=os.environ.get("IMAP_HOST", "imap.hostinger.com"),
    73→            imap_port=int(os.environ.get("IMAP_PORT", "993")),
    74→            imap_user=require("IMAP_USER"),
    75→            imap_pass=require("IMAP_PASS"),
    76→            smtp_host=os.environ.get("SMTP_HOST", "smtp.hostinger.com"),
    77→            smtp_port=int(os.environ.get("SMTP_PORT", "465")),
    78→            smtp_user=require("SMTP_USER"),
    79→            smtp_pass=require("SMTP_PASS"),
    80→            workspace=workspace,
    81→            ralph_sh=ralph_sh,
    82→            poll_interval=int(os.environ.get("AGENTX_POLL_INTERVAL", "30")),
    83→            allowed_senders=allowed_senders,
    84→            ralph_timeout=int(os.environ.get("AGENTX_RALPH_TIMEOUT", "1800")),
    85→        )
    86→
    87→
    88→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    89→# Email parsing
    90→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91→
    92→def extract_spec(msg: email.message.Message) -> str:
    93→    """Return markdown spec text from an email.
    94→
    95→    Preference order:
    96→    1. First .md attachment
    97→    2. text/plain body
    98→    3. text/html body stripped to plain text (fallback)
    99→    """
   100→    plain_body: str | None = None
   101→    html_body: str | None = None
   102→
   103→    for part in msg.walk():
   104→        content_type = part.get_content_type()
   105→        filename = part.get_filename() or ""
   106→        disposition = str(part.get("Content-Disposition", ""))
   107→
   108→        if "attachment" in disposition and filename.endswith(".md"):
   109→            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
   110→            if payload:
   111→                return payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
   112→
   113→        if content_type == "text/plain" and "attachment" not in disposition:
   114→            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
   115→            if payload:
   116→                plain_body = payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
   117→
   118→        elif content_type == "text/html" and "attachment" not in disposition:
   119→            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
   120→            if payload:
   121→                html_body = payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
   122→
   123→    if plain_body:
   124→        return plain_body
   125→    if html_body:
   126→        import re
   127→        text = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", html_body)
   128→        text = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", text)
   129→        return text.strip()
   130→    return ""
   131→
   132→
   133→def parse_subject(raw_subject: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
   134→    """Return (prefix, description) from a subject line.
   135→
   136→    Examples:
   137→        "[task] deploy harness" -> ("task", "deploy harness")
   138→        "[stop]"                -> ("stop", "")
   139→        "[status]"              -> ("status", "")
   140→    """
   141→    import re
   142→    m = re.match(r"^\[(\w+)\]\s*(.*)", raw_subject.strip(), re.IGNORECASE)
   143→    if m:
   144→        return m.group(1).lower(), m.group(2).strip()
   145→    return "", raw_subject.strip()
   146→
   147→
   148→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149→# Actions
   150→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   151→
   152→async def dispatch_task(cfg: Config, spec: str, description: str) -> str:
   153→    """Write spec into IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md and run ralph.sh.
   154→
   155→    The spec is prepended as a new task so ralph's normal plan-reading
   156→    flow picks it up on the next iteration.
   157→
   158→    Returns a human-readable work summary string.
   159→    """
   160→    plan_file = cfg.workspace / "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
   161→    task_block = f"- [ ] **Email task:** {description}\n\n{spec}\n\n"
   162→    if plan_file.exists():
   163→        existing = plan_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   164→        # Insert after the "## Current Focus" heading
   165→        marker = "## Current Focus"
   166→        if marker in existing:
   167→            plan_file.write_text(
   168→                existing.replace(marker, f"{marker}\n\n{task_block}", 1),
   169→                encoding="utf-8",
   170→            )
   171→        else:
   172→            plan_file.write_text(task_block + existing, encoding="utf-8")
   173→    else:
   174→        plan_file.write_text(
   175→            f"# Implementation Plan\n\n## Current Focus\n\n{task_block}",
   176→            encoding="utf-8",
   177→        )
   178→    log.info("Wrote task to %s", plan_file)
   179→
   180→    env = {**os.environ, "WORKSPACE_PATH": str(cfg.workspace)}
   181→    log.info("Launching ralph.sh at %s", cfg.ralph_sh)
   182→
   183→    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
   184→        str(cfg.ralph_sh),
   185→        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   186→        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   187→        env=env,
   188→        cwd=str(cfg.workspace),
   189→    )
   190→    try:
   191→        stdout, _ = await asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=cfg.ralph_timeout)
   192→    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   193→        proc.kill()
   194→        await proc.communicate()
   195→        log.error("ralph.sh timed out after %ds", cfg.ralph_timeout)
   196→        return f"Ralph loop timed out after {cfg.ralph_timeout // 60} minutes.\n\nTask: {description}"
   197→
   198→    output = stdout.decode("utf-8", errors="replace") if stdout else ""
   199→    exit_code = proc.returncode or 0
   200→    status = "completed" if exit_code == 0 else f"exited with code {exit_code}"
   201→    summary = (
   202→        f"Ralph loop {status}.\n\n"
   203→        f"Task: {description}\n\n"
   204→        f"--- Loop output (last 100 lines) ---\n"
   205→        + "\n".join(output.splitlines()[-100:])
   206→    )
   207→    log.info("Ralph finished (exit=%d)", exit_code)
   208→    return summary
   209→
   210→
   211→def get_status(cfg: Config) -> str:
   212→    """Return current loop state and recent git commits."""
   213→    lines: list[str] = ["agentx status\n"]
   214→
   215→    stop_file = cfg.workspace / ".stop"
   216→    if stop_file.exists():
   217→        lines.append("Stop sentinel is present — loop will exit after current iteration.\n")
   218→    else:
   219→        lines.append("No stop sentinel.\n")
   220→
   221→    try:
   222→        result = subprocess.run(
   223→            ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-10"],
   224→            cwd=str(cfg.workspace),
   225→            capture_output=True,
   226→            text=True,
   227→            timeout=10,
   228→        )
   229→        lines.append("\nRecent commits:\n")
   230→        lines.append(result.stdout or "(none)")
   231→    except Exception as exc:
   232→        lines.append(f"\nCould not read git log: {exc}")
   233→
   234→    return "\n".join(lines)
   235→
   236→
   237→def write_stop_sentinel(cfg: Config) -> None:
   238→    stop_file = cfg.workspace / ".stop"
   239→    stop_file.write_text("stop\n", encoding="utf-8")
   240→    log.info("Wrote stop sentinel to %s", stop_file)
   241→
   242→
   243→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   244→# SMTP reply
   245→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   246→
   247→async def send_reply(cfg: Config, to_addr: str, subject: str, body: str) -> None:
   248→    msg = EmailMessage()
   249→    msg["From"] = cfg.smtp_user
   250→    msg["To"] = to_addr
   251→    msg["Subject"] = f"Re: {subject}"
   252→    msg.set_content(body)
   253→
   254→    try:
   255→        await aiosmtplib.send(
   256→            msg,
   257→            hostname=cfg.smtp_host,
   258→            port=cfg.smtp_port,
   259→            username=cfg.smtp_user,
   260→            password=cfg.smtp_pass,
   261→            use_tls=True,
   262→        )
   263→        log.info("Replied to %s", to_addr)
   264→    except Exception as exc:
   265→        log.error("Failed to send reply to %s: %s", to_addr, exc)
   266→
   267→
   268→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   269→# IMAP polling
   270→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   271→
   272→async def process_message(cfg: Config, raw: bytes) -> None:
   273→    msg = email.message_from_bytes(raw, policy=email.policy.default)
   274→    from_addr = str(msg.get("From", ""))
   275→    subject = str(msg.get("Subject", ""))
   276→
   277→    log.info("Processing message from=%r subject=%r", from_addr, subject)
   278→
   279→    if cfg.allowed_senders:
   280→        import re as _re
   281→        # Extract bare address from "Name <addr>" format
   282→        m = _re.search(r"<([^>]+)>", from_addr)
   283→        bare = (m.group(1) if m else from_addr).lower().strip()
   284→        if bare not in cfg.allowed_senders:
   285→            log.warning("Rejected message from unauthorised sender %r", from_addr)
   286→            return
   287→
   288→    prefix, description = parse_subject(subject)
   289→
   290→    if prefix == "task":
   291→        spec = extract_spec(msg)
   292→        if not spec:
   293→            reply_body = "Could not extract a spec from your email. Please include a markdown spec in the body or attach a .md file."
   294→        else:
   295→            reply_body = await dispatch_task(cfg, spec, description)
   296→        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, reply_body)
   297→
   298→    elif prefix == "stop":
   299→        write_stop_sentinel(cfg)
   300→        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, "Stop sentinel written. The loop will exit cleanly after its current iteration.")
   301→
   302→    elif prefix == "status":
   303→        status_body = get_status(cfg)
   304→        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, status_body)
   305→
   306→    else:
   307→        log.debug("Ignoring message with unrecognized prefix %r", prefix)
   308→
   309→
   310→async def poll_once(cfg: Config, imap: aioimaplib.IMAP4_SSL) -> None:
   311→    """Search for UNSEEN messages and process each one."""
   312→    status, data = await imap.uid("search", "UNSEEN")
   313→    if status != "OK":
   314→        log.warning("IMAP SEARCH failed: %s %s", status, data)
   315→        return
   316→
   317→    uid_list_raw = data[0].decode() if isinstance(data[0], bytes) else str(data[0])
   318→    uids = [u for u in uid_list_raw.split() if u]
   319→    if not uids:
   320→        return
   321→
   322→    log.info("Found %d unseen message(s)", len(uids))
   323→
   324→    for uid in uids:
   325→        try:
   326→            fetch_status, fetch_data = await imap.uid("fetch", uid, "(RFC822)")
   327→            if fetch_status != "OK":
   328→                log.warning("FETCH failed for uid %s: %s", uid, fetch_status)
   329→                continue
   330→
   331→            # aioimaplib returns [metadata_line, message_bytes, b')']
   332→            # The actual RFC822 body is the largest bytes item in the response.
   333→            raw: bytes | None = None
   334→            candidates = [item for item in fetch_data if isinstance(item, bytes) and item != b")"]
   335→            if candidates:
   336→                raw = max(candidates, key=len)
   337→
   338→            if not raw:
   339→                log.warning("No body data for uid %s", uid)
   340→                continue
   341→
   342→            await process_message(cfg, raw)
   343→
   344→            await imap.uid("store", uid, "+FLAGS", r"(\Seen)")
   345→
   346→        except Exception as exc:
   347→            log.exception("Error processing uid %s: %s", uid, exc)
   348→
   349→
   350→async def run_listener(cfg: Config) -> None:
   351→    log.info("Connecting to IMAP %s:%d as %s", cfg.imap_host, cfg.imap_port, cfg.imap_user)
   352→
   353→    while True:
   354→        try:
   355→            imap = aioimaplib.IMAP4_SSL(host=cfg.imap_host, port=cfg.imap_port)
   356→            await imap.wait_hello_from_server()
   357→            await imap.login(cfg.imap_user, cfg.imap_pass)
   358→            await imap.select("INBOX")
   359→            log.info("IMAP connected; polling every %ds", cfg.poll_interval)
   360→
   361→            while True:
   362→                await poll_once(cfg, imap)
   363→                await asyncio.sleep(cfg.poll_interval)
   364→
   365→        except asyncio.CancelledError:
   366→            raise
   367→        except Exception as exc:
   368→            log.error("IMAP error: %s — reconnecting in 60s", exc)
   369→            await asyncio.sleep(60)
   370→
   371→
   372→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   373→# Entry point
   374→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   375→
   376→def main() -> None:
   377→    logging.basicConfig(
   378→        level=logging.INFO,
   379→        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s",
   380→        stream=sys.stdout,
   381→    )
   382→
   383→    try:
   384→        cfg = Config.from_env()
   385→    except RuntimeError as exc:
   386→        log.error("Configuration error: %s", exc)
   387→        sys.exit(1)
   388→
   389→    log.info("agentx listener starting (workspace=%s)", cfg.workspace)
   390→
   391→    try:
   392→        asyncio.run(run_listener(cfg))
   393→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   394→        log.info("Shutting down")
   395→
   396→
   397→if __name__ == "__main__":
   398→    main()
   399→

"""
agentx email listener

Polls agentx@runggp.com IMAP inbox for task emails, dispatches ralph.sh,
and replies with a work summary.

Usage:
    uv run --env-file /opt/agentx/secrets.env src/listener.py

Subject prefixes:
    [task] <description>   — extract spec from body/.md attachment, run ralph
    [stop]                 — write .stop sentinel to workspace; loop exits cleanly
    [status]               — reply with current loop state and recent git commits
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import email
import email.policy
import logging
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from email.message import EmailMessage
from pathlib import Path

import aioimaplib
import aiosmtplib

log = logging.getLogger("agentx.listener")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Config:
    imap_host: str
    imap_port: int
    imap_user: str
    imap_pass: str
    smtp_host: str
    smtp_port: int
    smtp_user: str
    smtp_pass: str
    workspace: Path
    ralph_sh: Path
    poll_interval: int = 30
    allowed_senders: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
    ralph_timeout: int = 1800

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> "Config":
        def require(key: str) -> str:
            val = os.environ.get(key, "")
            if not val:
                raise RuntimeError(f"Required env var {key!r} is not set")
            return val

        workspace = Path(os.environ.get("WORKSPACE_PATH", "/opt/agentx"))
        ralph_sh = Path(os.environ.get("RALPH_SH", str(workspace / "ralph.sh")))

        raw_senders = os.environ.get("AGENTX_ALLOWED_SENDERS", "")
        allowed_senders: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
            s.strip().lower() for s in raw_senders.split(",") if s.strip()
        )

        return cls(
            imap_host=os.environ.get("IMAP_HOST", "imap.hostinger.com"),
            imap_port=int(os.environ.get("IMAP_PORT", "993")),
            imap_user=require("IMAP_USER"),
            imap_pass=require("IMAP_PASS"),
            smtp_host=os.environ.get("SMTP_HOST", "smtp.hostinger.com"),
            smtp_port=int(os.environ.get("SMTP_PORT", "465")),
            smtp_user=require("SMTP_USER"),
            smtp_pass=require("SMTP_PASS"),
            workspace=workspace,
            ralph_sh=ralph_sh,
            poll_interval=int(os.environ.get("AGENTX_POLL_INTERVAL", "30")),
            allowed_senders=allowed_senders,
            ralph_timeout=int(os.environ.get("AGENTX_RALPH_TIMEOUT", "1800")),
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Email parsing
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def extract_spec(msg: email.message.Message) -> str:
    """Return markdown spec text from an email.

    Preference order:
    1. First .md attachment
    2. text/plain body
    3. text/html body stripped to plain text (fallback)
    """
    plain_body: str | None = None
    html_body: str | None = None

    for part in msg.walk():
        content_type = part.get_content_type()
        filename = part.get_filename() or ""
        disposition = str(part.get("Content-Disposition", ""))

        if "attachment" in disposition and filename.endswith(".md"):
            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
            if payload:
                return payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()

        if content_type == "text/plain" and "attachment" not in disposition:
            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
            if payload:
                plain_body = payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()

        elif content_type == "text/html" and "attachment" not in disposition:
            payload = part.get_payload(decode=True)
            if payload:
                html_body = payload.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()

    if plain_body:
        return plain_body
    if html_body:
        import re
        text = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", html_body)
        text = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", text)
        return text.strip()
    return ""


def parse_subject(raw_subject: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Return (prefix, description) from a subject line.

    Examples:
        "[task] deploy harness" -> ("task", "deploy harness")
        "[stop]"                -> ("stop", "")
        "[status]"              -> ("status", "")
    """
    import re
    m = re.match(r"^\[(\w+)\]\s*(.*)", raw_subject.strip(), re.IGNORECASE)
    if m:
        return m.group(1).lower(), m.group(2).strip()
    return "", raw_subject.strip()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Actions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def dispatch_task(cfg: Config, spec: str, description: str) -> str:
    """Write spec into IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md and run ralph.sh.

    The spec is prepended as a new task so ralph's normal plan-reading
    flow picks it up on the next iteration.

    Returns a human-readable work summary string.
    """
    plan_file = cfg.workspace / "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
    task_block = f"- [ ] **Email task:** {description}\n\n{spec}\n\n"
    if plan_file.exists():
        existing = plan_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        # Insert after the "## Current Focus" heading
        marker = "## Current Focus"
        if marker in existing:
            plan_file.write_text(
                existing.replace(marker, f"{marker}\n\n{task_block}", 1),
                encoding="utf-8",
            )
        else:
            plan_file.write_text(task_block + existing, encoding="utf-8")
    else:
        plan_file.write_text(
            f"# Implementation Plan\n\n## Current Focus\n\n{task_block}",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
    log.info("Wrote task to %s", plan_file)

    env = {**os.environ, "WORKSPACE_PATH": str(cfg.workspace)}
    log.info("Launching ralph.sh at %s", cfg.ralph_sh)

    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
        str(cfg.ralph_sh),
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
        env=env,
        cwd=str(cfg.workspace),
    )
    try:
        stdout, _ = await asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=cfg.ralph_timeout)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        proc.kill()
        await proc.communicate()
        log.error("ralph.sh timed out after %ds", cfg.ralph_timeout)
        return f"Ralph loop timed out after {cfg.ralph_timeout // 60} minutes.\n\nTask: {description}"

    output = stdout.decode("utf-8", errors="replace") if stdout else ""
    exit_code = proc.returncode or 0
    status = "completed" if exit_code == 0 else f"exited with code {exit_code}"
    summary = (
        f"Ralph loop {status}.\n\n"
        f"Task: {description}\n\n"
        f"--- Loop output (last 100 lines) ---\n"
        + "\n".join(output.splitlines()[-100:])
    )
    log.info("Ralph finished (exit=%d)", exit_code)
    return summary


def get_status(cfg: Config) -> str:
    """Return current loop state and recent git commits."""
    lines: list[str] = ["agentx status\n"]

    stop_file = cfg.workspace / ".stop"
    if stop_file.exists():
        lines.append("Stop sentinel is present — loop will exit after current iteration.\n")
    else:
        lines.append("No stop sentinel.\n")

    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "log", "--oneline", "-10"],
            cwd=str(cfg.workspace),
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=10,
        )
        lines.append("\nRecent commits:\n")
        lines.append(result.stdout or "(none)")
    except Exception as exc:
        lines.append(f"\nCould not read git log: {exc}")

    return "\n".join(lines)


def write_stop_sentinel(cfg: Config) -> None:
    stop_file = cfg.workspace / ".stop"
    stop_file.write_text("stop\n", encoding="utf-8")
    log.info("Wrote stop sentinel to %s", stop_file)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SMTP reply
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def send_reply(cfg: Config, to_addr: str, subject: str, body: str) -> None:
    msg = EmailMessage()
    msg["From"] = cfg.smtp_user
    msg["To"] = to_addr
    msg["Subject"] = f"Re: {subject}"
    msg.set_content(body)

    try:
        await aiosmtplib.send(
            msg,
            hostname=cfg.smtp_host,
            port=cfg.smtp_port,
            username=cfg.smtp_user,
            password=cfg.smtp_pass,
            use_tls=True,
        )
        log.info("Replied to %s", to_addr)
    except Exception as exc:
        log.error("Failed to send reply to %s: %s", to_addr, exc)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IMAP polling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def process_message(cfg: Config, raw: bytes) -> None:
    msg = email.message_from_bytes(raw, policy=email.policy.default)
    from_addr = str(msg.get("From", ""))
    subject = str(msg.get("Subject", ""))

    log.info("Processing message from=%r subject=%r", from_addr, subject)

    if cfg.allowed_senders:
        import re as _re
        # Extract bare address from "Name <addr>" format
        m = _re.search(r"<([^>]+)>", from_addr)
        bare = (m.group(1) if m else from_addr).lower().strip()
        if bare not in cfg.allowed_senders:
            log.warning("Rejected message from unauthorised sender %r", from_addr)
            return

    prefix, description = parse_subject(subject)

    if prefix == "task":
        spec = extract_spec(msg)
        if not spec:
            reply_body = "Could not extract a spec from your email. Please include a markdown spec in the body or attach a .md file."
        else:
            reply_body = await dispatch_task(cfg, spec, description)
        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, reply_body)

    elif prefix == "stop":
        write_stop_sentinel(cfg)
        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, "Stop sentinel written. The loop will exit cleanly after its current iteration.")

    elif prefix == "status":
        status_body = get_status(cfg)
        await send_reply(cfg, from_addr, subject, status_body)

    else:
        log.debug("Ignoring message with unrecognized prefix %r", prefix)


async def poll_once(cfg: Config, imap: aioimaplib.IMAP4_SSL) -> None:
    """Search for UNSEEN messages and process each one."""
    status, data = await imap.uid("search", "UNSEEN")
    if status != "OK":
        log.warning("IMAP SEARCH failed: %s %s", status, data)
        return

    uid_list_raw = data[0].decode() if isinstance(data[0], bytes) else str(data[0])
    uids = [u for u in uid_list_raw.split() if u]
    if not uids:
        return

    log.info("Found %d unseen message(s)", len(uids))

    for uid in uids:
        try:
            fetch_status, fetch_data = await imap.uid("fetch", uid, "(RFC822)")
            if fetch_status != "OK":
                log.warning("FETCH failed for uid %s: %s", uid, fetch_status)
                continue

            # aioimaplib returns [metadata_line, message_bytes, b')']
            # The actual RFC822 body is the largest bytes item in the response.
            raw: bytes | None = None
            candidates = [item for item in fetch_data if isinstance(item, bytes) and item != b")"]
            if candidates:
                raw = max(candidates, key=len)

            if not raw:
                log.warning("No body data for uid %s", uid)
                continue

            await process_message(cfg, raw)

            await imap.uid("store", uid, "+FLAGS", r"(\Seen)")

        except Exception as exc:
            log.exception("Error processing uid %s: %s", uid, exc)


async def run_listener(cfg: Config) -> None:
    log.info("Connecting to IMAP %s:%d as %s", cfg.imap_host, cfg.imap_port, cfg.imap_user)

    while True:
        try:
            imap = aioimaplib.IMAP4_SSL(host=cfg.imap_host, port=cfg.imap_port)
            await imap.wait_hello_from_server()
            await imap.login(cfg.imap_user, cfg.imap_pass)
            await imap.select("INBOX")
            log.info("IMAP connected; polling every %ds", cfg.poll_interval)

            while True:
                await poll_once(cfg, imap)
                await asyncio.sleep(cfg.poll_interval)

        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            raise
        except Exception as exc:
            log.error("IMAP error: %s — reconnecting in 60s", exc)
            await asyncio.sleep(60)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entry point
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main() -> None:
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.INFO,
        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s",
        stream=sys.stdout,
    )

    try:
        cfg = Config.from_env()
    except RuntimeError as exc:
        log.error("Configuration error: %s", exc)
        sys.exit(1)

    log.info("agentx listener starting (workspace=%s)", cfg.workspace)

    try:
        asyncio.run(run_listener(cfg))
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        log.info("Shutting down")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
## Summary of `/home/ralph/workspace/src/listener.py`

This file implements an email listener service for an AI agent system called "agentx". The service polls an email inbox for task emails and automatically dispatches a script called `ralph.sh` to execute the tasks.

### Major Classes and Functions:

#### **Config Class (Lines 39-86)**
- **Purpose**: Configuration management using environment variables
- **Key attributes**: IMAP/SMTP settings, workspace path, polling interval, allowed senders, timeouts
- **Key method**: `from_env()` - creates configuration from environment variables

#### **Email Parsing Functions (Lines 92-146)**

1. **`extract_spec(msg)`** (Lines 92-131)
   - **Purpose**: Extracts markdown specification from email messages
   - **Priority order**: .md attachment → plain text body → HTML body (stripped)

2. **`parse_subject(raw_subject)`** (Lines 133-146)
   - **Purpose**: Parses email subject lines to extract command prefixes and descriptions
   - **Supported prefixes**: `[task]`, `[stop]`, `[status]`

#### **Action Functions (Lines 152-241)**

1. **`dispatch_task(cfg, spec, description)`** (Lines 152-209)
   - **Purpose**: Main task execution function
   - **Actions**: Writes spec to `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, runs `ralph.sh`, returns work summary
   - **Timeout handling**: 30-minute timeout with graceful termination

2. **`get_status(cfg)`** (Lines 211-235)
   - **Purpose**: Returns current system status and recent git commits
   - **Output**: Stop sentinel status and last 10 git commits

3. **`write_stop_sentinel(cfg)`** (Lines 237-241)
   - **Purpose**: Creates a `.stop` file to signal clean shutdown

#### **Communication Functions (Lines 247-308)**

1. **`send_reply(cfg, to_addr, subject, body)`** (Lines 247-266)
   - **Purpose**: Sends email replies via SMTP
   - **Features**: Uses TLS encryption, error handling

2. **`process_message(cfg, raw)`** (Lines 272-308)
   - **Purpose**: Main message processing dispatcher
   - **Features**: Sender validation, command routing based on subject prefix

#### **IMAP Polling Functions (Lines 310-370)**

1. **`poll_once(cfg, imap)`** (Lines 310-348)
   - **Purpose**: Single polling cycle - searches for unseen messages and processes them
   - **Features**: UID-based message handling, automatic message marking as seen

2. **`run_listener(cfg)`** (Lines 350-370)
   - **Purpose**: Main event loop with automatic reconnection
   - **Features**: Continuous IMAP connection management, error recovery

#### **Entry Point (Lines 376-398)**

1. **`main()`** (Lines 376-395)
   - **Purpose**: Application entry point
   - **Features**: Logging setup, configuration loading, graceful shutdown

### Key Features:

1. **Email-driven task automation**: Processes emails with `[task]` prefix to execute automated workflows
2. **Multiple spec sources**: Accepts task specifications from .md attachments, plain text, or HTML email bodies
3. **Security**: Sender whitelist validation via `AGENTX_ALLOWED_SENDERS` environment variable
4. **Robustness**: Automatic IMAP reconnection, timeout handling, graceful error recovery
5. **Integration**: Writes tasks to `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` and executes `ralph.sh` for task processing
6. **Status monitoring**: Provides system status and git history via `[status]` command
7. **Clean shutdown**: Supports graceful termination via `[stop]` command

The service is designed to run continuously as a daemon, monitoring an email inbox and automatically executing AI agent tasks based on incoming emails.

    11→RED='\033[0;31m'
    12→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    13→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    14→CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    15→NC='\033[0m'
    16→
    17→log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[ralph]${NC} $1"; }
    18→log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[ralph]${NC} $1"; }
    19→log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ralph]${NC} $1"; }
    20→log_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[ralph]${NC} $1"; }
    21→
    22→# Generate work summary
    23→generate_work_summary() {
    24→    local exit_reason="${1:-completed}"
    25→    local session_end=$(date +%s)
    26→    local duration=$((session_end - SESSION_START))
    27→    local duration_mins=$((duration / 60))
    28→    local duration_secs=$((duration % 60))
    29→
    30→    echo ""
    31→    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    32→    if [ "$exit_reason" = "interrupted" ]; then
    33→        echo -e "  ${YELLOW}Ralph Session Interrupted${NC}"
    34→    elif [ "$exit_reason" = "error" ]; then
    35→        echo -e "  ${RED}Ralph Session Ended (Error)${NC}"
    36→    else
    37→        echo -e "  ${GREEN}Ralph Session Complete${NC}"
    38→    fi
    39→    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    40→    echo ""
    41→
    42→    # Session stats
    43→    echo "  Session Details:"
    44→    echo "    Branch: $RALPH_BRANCH"
    45→    echo "    Iterations: $ITERATION"
    46→    echo "    Duration: ${duration_mins}m ${duration_secs}s"
    47→    echo "    Mode: $MODE"
    48→    echo ""
    49→
    50→    # Git summary
    51→    local commit_count=$(git rev-list --count "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    52→    if [ "$commit_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    53→        echo "  Work Completed:"
    54→        echo "    Commits: $commit_count"
    55→
    56→        # Show files changed
    57→        local files_changed=$(git diff --name-only "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    58→        if [ "$files_changed" -gt 0 ]; then
    59→            echo "    Files modified: $files_changed"
    60→
    61→            # Show top 5 most changed files
    62→            echo ""
    63→            echo "    Key changes:"
    64→            git diff --stat "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null | head -6 | tail -5 | sed 's/^/      /'
    65→        fi
    66→
    67→        echo ""
    68→        echo "    Recent commits:"
    69→        git log --oneline "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | sed 's/^/      /'
    70→    else
    71→        echo "  No commits made during this session"
    72→    fi
    73→
    74→    # Implementation plan summary if exists
    75→    if [ -f "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" ]; then
    76→        local completed_count=$(grep -c "^## Completed" IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    77→        local current_count=$(grep -c "^## Current Focus" IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    78→        if [ "$completed_count" -gt 0 ] || [ "$current_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    79→            echo ""
    80→            echo "  Plan Status:"
    81→            echo "    See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md for details"
    82→        fi
    83→    fi
    84→
    85→    # Next steps
    86→    echo ""
    87→    echo "  Next Steps:"
    88→    if [ "$commit_count" -gt 0 ]; then
    89→        echo "    1. Review changes: git diff ${ORIGINAL_BRANCH}...${RALPH_BRANCH}"
    90→        echo "    2. Test the changes in your environment"
    91→        echo "    3. Create PR: gh pr create --base ${ORIGINAL_BRANCH} --head ${RALPH_BRANCH}"
    92→    else
    93→        echo "    1. Review any analysis or plans created"
    94→        echo "    2. Consider running: ./ralph.sh build"
    95→    fi
    96→
    97→    echo ""
    98→    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    99→}
   100→
   101→# Open a PR and immediately merge it into the base branch
   102→auto_merge_pr() {
   103→    local commit_count
   104→    commit_count=$(git rev-list --count "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   105→    if [ "$commit_count" -eq 0 ]; then
   106→        log_info "No commits on $RALPH_BRANCH — skipping PR"
   107→        return 0
   108→    fi
   109→
   110→    command -v gh &>/dev/null || { log_warn "gh CLI not found — skipping auto-merge"; return 0; }
   111→    [ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ] || { log_warn "GITHUB_TOKEN not set — skipping auto-merge"; return 0; }
   112→
   113→    local title
   114→    title="ralph: $(git log --oneline "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH" | tail -1 | cut -d' ' -f2-)"
   115→    local body
   116→    body="$(printf 'Auto-merged by Ralph (%d iteration(s))\n\n' "$ITERATION")$(git log --oneline "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH".."$RALPH_BRANCH")"
   117→
   118→    log_info "Creating PR: $RALPH_BRANCH -> $ORIGINAL_BRANCH"
   119→    local pr_url
   120→    if pr_url=$(gh pr create --base "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" --head "$RALPH_BRANCH" --title "$title" --body "$body" 2>&1); then
   121→        log_success "PR created: $pr_url"
   122→        if gh pr merge "$pr_url" --merge --delete-branch 2>&1; then
   123→            log_success "PR merged into $ORIGINAL_BRANCH"
   124→        else
   125→            log_warn "Auto-merge failed — review manually: $pr_url"
   126→        fi
   127→    else
   128→        log_warn "PR creation failed: $pr_url"
   129→    fi
   130→}
   131→
   132→# Cleanup scaffolding files
   133→cleanup_scaffolding() {
   134→    log_info "Cleaning up Ralph scaffolding files..."
   135→
   136→    local files_to_remove=()
   137→    local dirs_to_remove=()
   138→
   139→    # Check which scaffolding files exist
   140→    [ -d "specs" ] && dirs_to_remove+=("specs")
   141→    [ -f "AGENTS.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("AGENTS.md")
   142→    [ -f "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md")
   143→    [ -f "PROMPT_plan.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("PROMPT_plan.md")
   144→    [ -f "PROMPT_build.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("PROMPT_build.md")
   145→    [ -f "PROMPT_plan_local.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("PROMPT_plan_local.md")
   146→    [ -f "PROMPT_build_local.md" ] && files_to_remove+=("PROMPT_build_local.md")
   147→
   148→    if [ ${#files_to_remove[@]} -eq 0 ] && [ ${#dirs_to_remove[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
   149→        log_info "No scaffolding files to clean up"
   150→        return
   151→    fi
   152→
   153→    # Remove the files
   154→    for file in "${files_to_remove[@]}"; do
   155→        git rm -f "$file" 2>/dev/null || rm -f "$file"
   156→        log_info "  Removed: $file"
   157→    done
   158→
   159→    for dir in "${dirs_to_remove[@]}"; do
   160→        git rm -rf "$dir" 2>/dev/null || rm -rf "$dir"
   161→        log_info "  Removed: $dir/"
   162→    done
   163→
   164→    # Create cleanup commit if there are changes
   165→    if ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
   166→        log_info "Creating cleanup commit..."
   167→        git commit -m "chore: Remove Ralph scaffolding files
   168→
   169→These files were used during the Ralph development session
   170→and are no longer needed for the final pull request."
   171→
   172→        # Push if enabled
   173→        if [ "$PUSH_AFTER_COMMIT" = "true" ]; then
   174→            log_info "Pushing cleanup commit..."
   175→            git push origin "$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || \
   176→                git push -u origin "$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || \
   177→                log_warn "Push failed (continuing anyway)"
   178→        fi
   179→    fi
   180→}
   181→
   182→# Handle interruption gracefully
   183→handle_interruption() {
   184→    echo ""
   185→    log_warn "Ralph loop interrupted by user (Ctrl+C)"
   186→    generate_work_summary "interrupted"
   187→    /home/ralph/scripts/send-notification.sh "interrupted" "$RALPH_BRANCH" "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" "$ITERATION" "$(( $(date +%s) - SESSION_START ))"
   188→    exit 130
   189→}
   190→
   191→# Set up signal trap for graceful interruption
   192→trap 'handle_interruption' INT TERM
   193→
   194→# Check if Entire CLI is available and enabled
   195→entire_available() {
   196→    [ "${RALPH_ENTIRE_ENABLED:-false}" = "true" ] && command -v entire &>/dev/null
   197→}
   198→
   199→# Change to workspace directory
   200→cd "${RALPH_WORKSPACE:-/home/ralph/workspace}"
   201→
   202→# Configuration from environment
   203→MODE="${RALPH_MODE:-build}"
   204→MAX_ITERATIONS="${RALPH_MAX_ITERATIONS:-0}"
   205→MODEL="${RALPH_MODEL:-opus}"
   206→OUTPUT_FORMAT="${RALPH_OUTPUT_FORMAT:-plain}"
   207→PUSH_AFTER_COMMIT="${RALPH_PUSH_AFTER_COMMIT:-true}"
   208→SPEND_CEILING_USD="${RALPH_SPEND_CEILING_USD:-0}"
   209→
   210→# Determine prompt file
   211→case "$MODE" in
   212→    plan)
   213→        PROMPT_FILE="/home/ralph/prompts/PROMPT_plan.md"
   214→        ;;
   215→    build|*)
   216→        PROMPT_FILE="/home/ralph/prompts/PROMPT_build.md"
   217→        ;;
   218→esac
   219→
   220→# Check if using local model (Ollama) - prefer _local variants
   221→IS_LOCAL_MODEL=false
   222→if [[ "$MODEL" == ollama/* ]] || [[ -n "${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL:-}" && "${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL:-}" != *"anthropic.com"* ]]; then
   223→    IS_LOCAL_MODEL=true
   224→fi
   225→
   226→# Check for project-specific prompts (prefer _local for local models)
   227→if [ "$IS_LOCAL_MODEL" = true ] && [ -f "PROMPT_${MODE}_local.md" ]; then
   228→    PROMPT_FILE="PROMPT_${MODE}_local.md"
   229→    log_info "Using local model prompt: $PROMPT_FILE"
   230→elif [ -f "PROMPT_${MODE}.md" ]; then
   231→    PROMPT_FILE="PROMPT_${MODE}.md"
   232→    log_info "Using project prompt: $PROMPT_FILE"
   233→fi
   234→
   235→# Verify prompt exists
   236→if [ ! -f "$PROMPT_FILE" ]; then
   237→    log_error "Prompt file not found: $PROMPT_FILE"
   238→    exit 1
   239→fi
   240→
   241→# Read prompt content now (before any git operations that might stash/remove files)
   242→PROMPT_CONTENT=$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")
   243→
   244→# Build model argument
   245→MODEL_ARG="--model $MODEL"
   246→
   247→# Iteration counter
   248→ITERATION=0
   249→
   250→# Verify we're in a git repo
   251→if ! git rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
   252→    log_error "Not a git repository!"
   253→    log_error "Initialize with: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit'"
   254→    exit 1
   255→fi
   256→
   257→# Get the workspace name from directory
   258→WORKSPACE_NAME=$(basename "$(pwd)")
   259→
   260→# Create a new branch for this Ralph session
   261→TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
   262→RALPH_BRANCH="ralph/${WORKSPACE_NAME}-${TIMESTAMP}"
   263→ORIGINAL_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo "HEAD")
   264→
   265→# Guard: if we're on a stale ralph branch, switch to main before proceeding
   266→if [[ "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" == ralph/* ]]; then
   267→    log_warn "Currently on stale ralph branch: $ORIGINAL_BRANCH"
   268→    log_info "Switching to main before starting new session..."
   269→    DEFAULT_BRANCH=$(git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null | sed 's|refs/remotes/origin/||' || echo "main")
   270→    if ! git diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
   271→        log_warn "Committing uncommitted changes before switching branches..."
   272→        git add -A && git commit -m "wip: uncommitted changes from previous session" 2>/dev/null || true
   273→        git push origin "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || true
   274→    fi
   275→    if git checkout "$DEFAULT_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null; then
   276→        ORIGINAL_BRANCH="$DEFAULT_BRANCH"
   277→        log_info "Switched to $ORIGINAL_BRANCH"
   278→    else
   279→        log_error "Failed to switch to $DEFAULT_BRANCH — run: git checkout main && git pull"
   280→        exit 1
   281→    fi
   282→fi
   283→
   284→# Warn if main is behind origin so we don't silently work on stale code
   285→if git fetch origin "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" --dry-run 2>/dev/null; then
   286→    LOCAL=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)
   287→    REMOTE=$(git rev-parse "origin/$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || true)
   288→    if [ -n "$REMOTE" ] && [ "$LOCAL" != "$REMOTE" ]; then
   289→        BEHIND=$(git rev-list --count HEAD.."origin/$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   290→        if [ "$BEHIND" -gt 0 ]; then
   291→            log_warn "Branch is $BEHIND commit(s) behind origin/$ORIGINAL_BRANCH — consider running: git pull"
   292→        fi
   293→    fi
   294→fi
   295→
   296→# Check for uncommitted changes
   297→DID_STASH=false
   298→if ! git diff-index --quiet HEAD -- 2>/dev/null; then
   299→    log_warn "Uncommitted changes detected"
   300→    log_info "Stashing changes before creating branch..."
   301→    git stash push -m "Ralph auto-stash before $RALPH_BRANCH"
   302→    DID_STASH=true
   303→fi
   304→
   305→# Create and checkout the new branch
   306→log_info "Creating branch: $RALPH_BRANCH"
   307→if ! git checkout -b "$RALPH_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null; then
   308→    log_error "Failed to create branch $RALPH_BRANCH"
   309→    exit 1
   310→fi
   311→
   312→# Capture HEAD before any agent commits so iteration diffs are scoped correctly
   313→SESSION_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   314→
   315→# Restore stashed changes onto the new branch so scaffolding files are available
   316→if [ "$DID_STASH" = true ]; then
   317→    log_info "Restoring stashed changes onto $RALPH_BRANCH..."
   318→    git stash pop 2>/dev/null || log_warn "Stash pop failed (changes may conflict)"
   319→fi
   320→
   321→log_info "Starting loop..."
   322→log_info "Prompt: $PROMPT_FILE"
   323→log_info "Model: $MODEL"
   324→log_info "Branch: $RALPH_BRANCH (from $ORIGINAL_BRANCH)"
   325→log_info "Working dir: $(pwd)"
   326→
   327→SESSION_ID="$TIMESTAMP"
   328→if node /home/ralph/workspace/lib/session-logger.js init \
   329→    --session-id "$SESSION_ID" \
   330→    --branch "$RALPH_BRANCH" \
   331→    --original-branch "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" \
   332→    --mode "$MODE" \
   333→    --model "$MODEL" \
   334→    --max-iterations "$MAX_ITERATIONS" \
   335→    --head-at-start "$SESSION_HEAD" 2>/dev/null; then
   336→    log_info "Session log: logs/sessions/${SESSION_ID}.json"
   337→else
   338→    log_warn "Session logging unavailable (continuing without it)"
   339→fi
   340→
   341→# Check for IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
   342→if [ -f "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" ]; then
   343→    log_info "Found IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md - will continue from existing progress"
   344→else
   345→    log_warn "No IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md found - Ralph will create one"
   346→fi
   347→echo ""
   348→
   349→# Temp file for capturing output
   350→OUTPUT_TMP=$(mktemp)
   351→trap 'rm -f "$OUTPUT_TMP"' EXIT
   352→
   353→# Output formatting command
   354→format_output() {
   355→    if [ "$OUTPUT_FORMAT" = "pretty" ]; then
   356→        /home/ralph/scripts/format-output.sh
   357→    else
   358→        cat
   359→    fi
   360→}
   361→
   362→# Check for critical errors in output
   363→# Only check the result JSON line, not the full conversation
   364→check_for_errors() {
   365→    local output_file="$1"
   366→
   367→    # Extract just the final result line (contains "type":"result")
   368→    local result_line
   369→    result_line=$(grep '"type":"result"' "$output_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   370→
   371→    # If no result line, check for startup errors in full output
   372→    if [[ -z "$result_line" ]]; then
   373→        # Check for model not found during startup (LiteLLM specific error)
   374→        if grep -q "OllamaException.*not found\|litellm.*model.*not found" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   375→            log_error "Model not found: $MODEL"
   376→            echo ""
   377→            log_error "Available models - check litellm-config.yaml or run: ollama list"
   378→            return 1
   379→        fi
   380→
   381→        # Check for connection errors
   382→        if grep -q "APIConnectionError\|Connection refused\|ECONNREFUSED" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   383→            log_error "Connection error - is Ollama running?"
   384→            return 1
   385→        fi
   386→    fi
   387→
   388→    # Check result line for errors
   389→    if [[ -n "$result_line" ]]; then
   390→        if echo "$result_line" | grep -q '"is_error":true'; then
   391→            # Extract error message if present
   392→            local errors
   393→            errors=$(echo "$result_line" | grep -o '"errors":\[[^]]*\]' | head -1)
   394→            if [[ -n "$errors" ]]; then
   395→                log_warn "Session completed with errors"
   396→                # Don't fail - Ralph might have partially succeeded
   397→            fi
   398→        fi
   399→    fi
   400→
   401→    return 0
   402→}
   403→
   404→# Main loop
   405→log_info "Max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS (ITERATION starts at $ITERATION)"
   406→while true; do
   407→    # Check iteration limit
   408→    if [ "$MAX_ITERATIONS" -gt 0 ] && [ "$ITERATION" -ge "$MAX_ITERATIONS" ]; then
   409→        echo ""
   410→        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
   411→        log_success "Reached max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
   412→        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
   413→        break
   414→    fi
   415→
   416→    ITERATION=$((ITERATION + 1))
   417→    echo ""
   418→    echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
   419→    echo "│  ITERATION $ITERATION                                  │"
   420→    echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
   421→    echo ""
   422→
   423→    ITER_START_MS=$(date +%s%3N)
   424→    ITER_PREV_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   425→
   426→    # Run Claude with the prompt, capture output for error checking
   427→    # -p: Headless mode (non-interactive)
   428→    # --dangerously-skip-permissions: Auto-approve tool calls
   429→    # --output-format=stream-json: Structured output for filtering
   430→    echo "$PROMPT_CONTENT" | claude -p \
   431→        --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   432→        --output-format=stream-json \
   433→        $MODEL_ARG \
   434→        --verbose 2>&1 | tee "$OUTPUT_TMP" | format_output
   435→
   436→    CLAUDE_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
   437→    ITER_END_MS=$(date +%s%3N)
   438→    ITER_DURATION_MS=$((ITER_END_MS - ITER_START_MS))
   439→
   440→    # Log structured iteration data (non-blocking)
   441→    node /home/ralph/workspace/lib/session-logger.js iteration \
   442→        --session-id "$SESSION_ID" \
   443→        --iteration "$ITERATION" \
   444→        --model "$MODEL" \
   445→        --duration-ms "$ITER_DURATION_MS" \
   446→        --prev-commit "$ITER_PREV_COMMIT" \
   447→        "$OUTPUT_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
   448→
   449→    # Check spend ceiling
   450→    if [ "$SPEND_CEILING_USD" != "0" ] && [ -n "$SPEND_CEILING_USD" ]; then
   451→        spend_output=$(node /home/ralph/workspace/lib/session-logger.js check-spend \
   452→            --session-id "$SESSION_ID" \
   453→            --ceiling "$SPEND_CEILING_USD" 2>/dev/null)
   454→        spend_exit=$?
   455→        if [ $spend_exit -eq 2 ]; then
   456→            log_warn "Spend ceiling reached: $spend_output"
   457→            generate_work_summary "error"
   458→            /home/ralph/scripts/send-notification.sh "spend-limit" "$RALPH_BRANCH" "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" "$ITERATION" "$(( $(date +%s) - SESSION_START ))"
   459→            exit 1
   460→        fi
   461→    fi
   462→
   463→    # Check for critical errors
   464→    if ! check_for_errors "$OUTPUT_TMP"; then
   465→        log_error "Critical error detected, stopping loop"
   466→        echo ""
   467→        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
   468→        echo "  Raw error output:"
   469→        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
   470→        # Show last 50 lines of raw output for debugging
   471→        tail -50 "$OUTPUT_TMP"
   472→        generate_work_summary "error"
   473→        exit 1
   474→    fi
   475→
   476→    if [ "$CLAUDE_EXIT" -ne 0 ]; then
   477→        log_warn "Claude exited with code $CLAUDE_EXIT"
   478→        # Show some context on non-zero exit
   479→        echo "Last output:"
   480→        tail -20 "$OUTPUT_TMP"
   481→    fi
   482→
   483→    # Push changes if configured
   484→    if [ "$PUSH_AFTER_COMMIT" = "true" ]; then
   485→        CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   486→        if [ -n "$CURRENT_BRANCH" ]; then
   487→            log_info "Pushing to origin/$CURRENT_BRANCH..."
   488→            push_output=""
   489→            if push_output=$(git push origin "$CURRENT_BRANCH" 2>&1); then
   490→                log_success "Push successful"
   491→            elif push_output=$(git push -u origin "$CURRENT_BRANCH" 2>&1); then
   492→                log_success "Push successful (set upstream)"
   493→            else
   494→                log_warn "Push failed (continuing anyway)"
   495→                # Check for common SSH issues
   496→                if echo "$push_output" | grep -q "authenticity of host\|Host key verification"; then
   497→                    log_warn "SSH host key not trusted. Run on host machine:"
   498→                    log_warn "  ssh-keyscan <git-host> >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts"
   499→                elif echo "$push_output" | grep -q "Permission denied\|publickey"; then
   500→                    log_warn "SSH key issue. Ensure ~/.ssh is mounted and keys have correct permissions"
   501→                else
   502→                    echo "$push_output" | head -5
   503→                fi
   504→            fi
   505→        fi
   506→    else
   507→        log_info "Push disabled (RALPH_PUSH_AFTER_COMMIT=false)"
   508→    fi
   509→
   510→    echo ""
   511→    echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════"
   512→    log_success "ITERATION $ITERATION COMPLETE"
   513→    if entire_available; then
   514→        checkpoint_info=$(entire status --short 2>/dev/null) && \
   515→            log_info "Entire: $checkpoint_info" || true
   516→    fi
   517→    echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════"
   518→    echo ""
   519→
   520→    # Check if all tasks in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md are complete
   521→    if [ -f "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" ]; then
   522→        pending_tasks=$(grep -c '^\s*- \[ \]' IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   523→        completed_tasks=$(grep -c '^\s*- \[x\]' IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   524→        if [ "$pending_tasks" -eq 0 ] && [ "$completed_tasks" -gt 0 ]; then
   525→            echo ""
   526→            log_success "All tasks complete ($completed_tasks done, 0 pending) — stopping early"
   527→            break
   528→        else
   529→            log_info "Plan status: $completed_tasks done, $pending_tasks pending"
   530→        fi
   531→    fi
   532→
   533→    # Small delay between iterations
   534→    sleep 1
   535→done
   536→
   537→# Generate work summary
   538→generate_work_summary "completed"
   539→
   540→# Clean up scaffolding files if configured
   541→if [ "${RALPH_CLEANUP_SCAFFOLDING:-true}" = "true" ]; then
   542→    cleanup_scaffolding
   543→else
   544→    log_info "Scaffolding cleanup disabled (RALPH_CLEANUP_SCAFFOLDING=false)"
   545→fi
   546→
   547→auto_merge_pr
   548→
   549→/home/ralph/scripts/send-notification.sh "completed" "$RALPH_BRANCH" "$ORIGINAL_BRANCH" "$ITERATION" "$(( $(date +%s) - SESSION_START ))"
   550→
   551→echo ""
   552→log_success "Loop finished after $ITERATION iterations"
   553→
